iconic_detail: Crew found dead, faces frozen in terror

chapters.voiceover:
June 1947, the Strait of Malacca. Two American ships pick up a distress call from a Dutch freighter, the S.S. Ourang Medan.

"All officers including the captain, dead. Lying in the chartroom and on the bridge."

Then one last transmission: "I die."

A rescue crew reaches the ship. Every man aboard is dead. No wounds. No fire. Faces frozen in what witnesses called pure terror. Before a tow line goes on, the ship reportedly explodes and sinks, taking every scrap of evidence with it.

Here is the problem. No shipping registry has ever listed a vessel named Ourang Medan. No wreck. No crew list. No original radio log survives. Only retellings.

So was this ever a real ship, or a story that just would not sink?
